items.title: Chrome V8 Zero-Day CVE-2026-11645 Exploited in the Wild — Patch Now
items.summary: Google patched a high-severity out-of-bounds memory access bug (CVSS 8.8) in Chrome's V8 JavaScript/WebAssembly engine. An exploit is confirmed active in the wild, making this the latest in a string of Chrome zero-days weaponized in 2026.
items.why: Actively exploited zero-days in the world's dominant browser demand immediate patching — every unpatched desktop is a live attack surface. This is the fourth Chrome zero-day exploited in the wild this year alone.

items.title: Critical Check Point VPN Zero-Day CVE-2026-50751 Under Active Exploitation
items.summary: A critical zero-day in Check Point VPN is being actively exploited, with Rapid7 tracking confirmed attack activity since May 7, 2026 and a notable spike in early June. The vulnerability enables unauthorized remote access to corporate network perimeters.
items.why: VPN appliance vulnerabilities are a top initial-access vector for ransomware groups and nation-state actors. Active exploitation in early June means incident responders should treat unpatched Check Point gateways as potentially compromised.

items.title: APT28 Weaponizes Microsoft Office 1-Day CVE-2026-21509 With Cloud C2 Infrastructure
items.summary: Trellix researchers detail a stealthy multi-stage APT28 (Fancy Bear) campaign targeting European military and government entities. The Russian GRU-linked group weaponized a Microsoft Office vulnerability within 24 hours of public disclosure and uses cloud services as C2 to blend into legitimate traffic.
items.why: APT28's sub-24-hour exploitation of Office flaws and use of cloud C2 blending highlights the speed at which nation-state actors operationalize disclosures — defenders must treat patch SLAs as hours, not days.

items.title: Iran-Nexus APT 'Screening Serpens' Escalates Espionage Ops Across Five Countries
items.summary: Palo Alto Unit 42 is tracking an Iranian APT group dubbed Screening Serpens that has significantly expanded operations since a regional conflict began in February 2026. The group deploys two novel RAT families against targets in five countries, with a focus on government and critical-infrastructure sectors.
items.why: Iranian APT tempo has markedly increased in 2026 with new custom malware families — defenders in targeted regions (Middle East, Europe) should update detections and hunt for the newly documented RAT IOCs.

items.title: CrowdStrike 2026 Global Threat Report: eCrime Breakout Time Hits 27 Seconds as AI Fuels 89% Surge
items.summary: CrowdStrike's annual threat report reveals the average eCrime lateral-movement breakout time dropped to 29 minutes (fastest ever: 27 seconds) and AI-enabled adversary operations surged 89% year-over-year. Adversaries are injecting malicious prompts into GenAI tools and abusing AI dev platforms at over 90 organizations.
items.why: The 27-second breakout benchmark shatters the assumption that defenders have time to detect and respond post-compromise — organizations without automated detection and response are already too slow by any human-reaction standard.
